# Uploading the dataset to the EBV Data Portal {#sec-uploading}

By the end of this unit, you will be able to:

-   return to the portal upload form you started in [@sec-preparing];
-   upload your validated netCDF and a preview image;
-   submit the dataset for review and follow it through to publication;
-   find your dataset's DOI and citation, and publish later versions.

By this point the hard part is done. In [@sec-preparing] you filled the portal upload form and downloaded the preliminary metadata, and in [@sec-creating] and [@sec-validating] you built the netCDF and checked it. This unit closes the loop: go back to the portal, upload the file, and submit it for review. It is mostly clicking, so it is short.

## Back to the upload form

The submission uses the same New Upload form you met in [@sec-preparing]. Sign in with your GEO BON account and reopen your draft under **Uploads**. The *General information* and *EBV attributes* tabs are already filled from earlier, so the only tab left to finish is *Data upload*.

## Uploading your netCDF

On the *Data upload* tab the portal offers two ways to submit: a ZIP of GeoTIFFs, or a netCDF built with the `ebvcube` package. You built the netCDF, so choose that option and upload your `.nc` file.

If the form asks for a preview image, add one. A small map of a single slice, exported from `ebv_map()`, is enough and makes your dataset easier to recognize in the catalogue. The portal runs its own validation when you upload; if it flags anything, work back through the checks in [@sec-validating], fix the file, and upload it again.

## Submitting for review

Once the file is in, submit the dataset for review. It moves out of *Draft* into a review state, where the portal team checks the metadata and the file against the EBV standard before it goes public. Expect a wait, and possibly a request for small changes. When it is approved, the dataset is published: it appears in the public catalogue and becomes downloadable with `ebv_download()`.

## After publication: DOI, citation, and versions

On publication the portal assigns your dataset a DOI and generates its citation, both shown on the dataset page. You can see this on the live page for the running example, [dataset id = 1](https://portal.geobon.org/ebv-detail?id=1), where its version history, DOI, and citation all appear. Point people to those when they cite your data, not to the `ebvcube` package citation, which credits the software rather than the dataset.

If you later revise the data, upload it as a new version instead of editing the published one. The portal keeps versions distinct, so anyone who already cited the earlier DOI still lands on the exact data they used.

Here is that page, embedded live (in the HTML version of the book):

<iframe src="https://portal.geobon.org/ebv-detail?id=1"
        title="EBV Data Portal page for dataset id = 1 (Local bird diversity, cSAR/BES-SIM)"
        width="100%" height="600"
        style="border:1px solid #ddd; border-radius:6px;"
        loading="lazy">
</iframe>

<p>If the page does not load, <a href="https://portal.geobon.org/ebv-detail?id=1">open dataset id = 1 on the EBV Data Portal</a>.</p>

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**Checkpoint.** Your netCDF is uploaded, submitted, and, once approved, live on the EBV Data Portal with its own DOI. If anything snagged along the way, [@sec-troubleshooting] collects the fixes for the problems that come up most often.
:::
